analysis.core_story_summary: Rituals can be categorized in to two types- 1) Rituals performed with the objective of fulfilling worldly desires. 2) Rituals pertaining to Ultra mundan desires. In general, rituals can be classified into five categories - rituals, penance, chanting of mantras, meditation, and all the four collectively.

audience_notes.children_8_12: Use simple language, explain divine names as characters in a sacred legend.
audience_notes.teens_13_17: Connect events to decision-making, identity, and consequences.
audience_notes.young_adults_18_30: Highlight ethics, leadership, and social relevance.
audience_notes.devotional_traditional: Preserve sacred framing and theological terms respectfully.
audience_notes.global_general: Keep cultural context brief and avoid insider assumptions.

sensitivity_notes: Contains conflict imagery that may need age-appropriate framing.

redesigned_story_by_demographic.children_8_12: **Desirable Rituals (Kamya Karma) — Kids (8-12):** Rituals can be categorized in to two types- 1) Rituals performed with the objective of fulfilling worldly desires. 2) Rituals pertaining to Ultra mundan desires. In general, rituals can be classified into five categories - rituals, penance, chanting of mantras, meditation, and all the four collectively. This story teaches dedication and sacrifice, the cost of conflict, inner focus and self-mastery. Think of it like a legend that helps us choose kindness, courage, and respect.
